backdrops.note: This opening act grabs attention and introduces the protagonist in their normal world. It sets tone and genre while hinting at the central conflict to come.
backdrops.title: Act One : The Hook

backdrops.note: A key event disrupts the protagonist’s life and presents a problem or opportunity. This is where the main story truly begins to take shape.
backdrops.title: Act Two : The Inciting Incident

backdrops.note: The protagonist makes a decisive choice that commits them to the journey. They leave their comfort zone, and the stakes become clear.
backdrops.title: Act Three : The First Plot Point

backdrops.note: The protagonist actively pursues their goal, encountering obstacles, allies, and enemies. Tension builds steadily as complications arise.
backdrops.title: Act Four : Rising Action

backdrops.note: A major turning point shifts the story—often a revelation, reversal, or significant success/failure. It raises the stakes and changes the protagonist’s approach.
backdrops.title: Act Five : Midpoint

backdrops.note: Everything seems to fall apart. The protagonist faces their greatest setback or internal conflict, often questioning their ability to succeed.
backdrops.title: Act Six : Crisis (or Low Point)

backdrops.note: The protagonist makes a final push to resolve the conflict, leading to the climax. Afterward, the story concludes with a resolution that shows the outcome and lasting impact of the journey.
backdrops.title: Act Seven : Climax & Resolution
